# LLMSpec Design

## Overview

`.llmspec.yaml` is an OpenAPI-equivalent structured format for LLM consumption. It is the primary orientation artifact for any indexed repo — compact, structured, and queryable by section. It sits at the repo root alongside `CLAUDE.md` and `llms.txt`.

---

## Full Schema

```yaml
# .llmspec.yaml — generated by codebase-indexer
# Format version: 1.0

project: string           # Repo/package name
version: string           # Current version (from package.json or tag)
description: string       # One sentence: what this project does

stack: string[]           # [typescript, react, postgres, express, ...]

entry_points:
  - path: string          # Relative path from repo root
    role: string          # Human description: "server entry", "route definitions", etc.

concepts:                 # Domain terms an LLM must know to work correctly
  - name: string
    definition: string    # One sentence

patterns:                 # Conventions an LLM must follow when editing
  - name: string
    files: string         # Path or glob indicating where this pattern applies
    convention: string    # What to do (imperative)

api_surface:              # Key public functions/endpoints worth knowing
  - name: string
    path: string          # File path
    io: string            # L1 notation: result = fn(input)
    flow: string          # L2 notation: step1 → step2 → output

doc_layers:
  design: string          # Path to design docs / ADRs
  code: string            # Source root
  public: string[]        # [README.md, docs/guides/, etc.]

shortcuts:                # Key commands
  dev: string             # Start dev server
  test: string            # Run tests
  build: string           # Build for production
  index: string           # Re-run codebase indexer
  [key]: string           # Any additional commands
```

---

## Generation Rules

### Auto-populated on first index

| Field | Source |
|---|---|
| `project` | `package.json` name, or repo directory name |
| `version` | `package.json` version, or "0.0.0" |
| `stack` | Detected from package files |
| `entry_points` | Files named `index`, `main`, `app`, `server`, `router` |
| `shortcuts` | `package.json` scripts, Makefile targets |
| `doc_layers.code` | Source root (`src/` if present, else repo root) |
| `doc_layers.public` | `README.md` if present |

### Requires human review

| Field | Why |
|---|---|
| `description` | One sentence summary requires judgment |
| `concepts` | Domain terms aren't in code |
| `patterns` | Conventions require interpretation |
| `api_surface` | Which functions are "key" requires judgment |
| `doc_layers.design` | Design doc location varies |

Auto-generated values for these fields are marked `"TODO: ..."` to signal they need review.

### Preservation on re-index

If `.llmspec.yaml` already exists, it is **never overwritten**. The indexer writes updated data to `.index/` and the agent is responsible for merging relevant changes into the spec manually.

---

## Querying by Section

The `get_llmspec` MCP tool returns the full spec or a named section:

```
get_llmspec()                    → full YAML (~500 tokens typical)
get_llmspec("entry_points")      → entry_points array only (~30 tokens)
get_llmspec("shortcuts")         → shortcuts map (~20 tokens)
get_llmspec("concepts")          → concepts array only
get_llmspec("patterns")          → patterns array only
get_llmspec("api_surface")       → api_surface array only
```

Agents requesting a single section spend 90% fewer tokens than loading the full spec.

---

## Relationship to Other Artifacts

| Artifact | Relationship to LLMSpec |
|---|---|
| `llms.txt` | Plain-text summary derived from LLMSpec + README |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Claude Code context, references LLMSpec sections |
| `.index/symbol-map.json` | Detailed symbol data; LLMSpec `api_surface` is a curated subset |
| `.index/patterns.md` | Full patterns detail; LLMSpec `patterns` is a curated summary |
| `.index/shortcuts.md` | Full shortcuts; LLMSpec `shortcuts` is the key subset |

The LLMSpec is curated and stable. The `.index/` files are generated and exhaustive.

---

## Example

```yaml
project: billing-service
version: 2.3.1
description: Processes subscription payments and generates invoices for the SaaS platform.

stack: [typescript, node, postgres, stripe]

entry_points:
  - path: src/index.ts
    role: HTTP server entry point
  - path: src/workers/invoice.ts
    role: Background invoice generation worker

concepts:
  - name: Subscription
    definition: A recurring billing agreement between a customer and a plan, with a status lifecycle.
  - name: Invoice
    definition: A generated PDF record of charges for a billing period, associated with a subscription.

patterns:
  - name: Repository pattern
    files: src/repositories/
    convention: All database access goes through repository classes. Never query the DB directly from service or handler code.
  - name: Result type
    files: src/
    convention: Functions that can fail return Result<T, E> rather than throwing. Use isOk() and isErr() to handle.

api_surface:
  - name: createSubscription
    path: src/services/subscriptions.ts
    io: subscription = createSubscription({ customerId, planId, trialDays? })
    flow: validate plan → create Stripe subscription → persist to DB → emit subscription.created event
  - name: generateInvoice
    path: src/services/invoices.ts
    io: invoice = generateInvoice(subscriptionId, billingPeriod)
    flow: fetch subscription → calculate charges → render PDF → store → return invoice

doc_layers:
  design: docs/plans/
  code: src/
  public: [README.md, docs/api/]

shortcuts:
  dev: npm run dev
  test: npm test
  build: npm run build
  index: npm run index
  migrate: npm run db:migrate
```
